sourceMovie.title: Materialists
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Materialists works as a romance movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The romance and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Manhattan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Manhattan (1979) operates in the same romance as Materialists, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Before We Go
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Before We Go (2014) belongs to the same conversation as Materialists — both anchor their romance in drama emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: It Ends with Us
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Materialists's romance stayed with you, It Ends with Us (2024) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The drama through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Emma
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Materialists excels in romance, Emma (1996) doubles down on drama to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Frankie and Johnny
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Frankie and Johnny (1991) is the natural next step after Materialists: same romance DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and drama elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Corpse Bride
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Materialists who respond to its romance will find Corpse Bride (2005) immediately familiar. Both films share a fantasy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Whatever Works
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Materialists fans keep landing on Whatever Works (2009) — the romance is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Little Manhattan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Little Manhattan draws from the same romance well as Materialists, channeling comedy in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2005 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Lady from Shanghai
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Lady from Shanghai (1947) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Materialists later expanded. If Materialists resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: The Believer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama appeal of Materialists — that tense edge, the craft — translates cleanly into The Believer (2001). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

faq.question: What should I watch after Materialists?
faq.answer: Try Manhattan, Before We Go, It Ends with Us—each shares DNA with Materialists while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Materialists?
faq.answer: Materialists (2025) is best described as Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Materialists worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
